title: Cascading Failure Isolation
constraints: Each agent must run in its own isolated error boundary
If one agent crashes, the others must continue executing
The final output must include results from all successful agents and a failure note for crashed ones
The pipeline must not re-raise exceptions from individual agent failures
hints: Wrap each agent's execution in a try/except block that captures the error and stores a fallback result
Run agents in parallel if possible, or sequentially with isolated error handling per step
Aggregate results at the end: include successful outputs and clearly mark which agents failed

starterCode.langgraph:
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
from typing import TypedDict

class State(TypedDict):
    topic: str
    research: str
    analysis: str
    visualization: str
    report: str

def research_agent(state: State) -> State:
    return {"research": f"Research findings on {state['topic']}: [detailed research]"}

def analysis_agent(state: State) -> State:
    # BUG: This agent crashes and takes down the entire pipeline
    raise RuntimeError("Analysis model failed to load")

def visualization_agent(state: State) -> State:
    return {"visualization": f"Charts generated for {state['topic']}"}

def report_agent(state: State) -> State:
    return {"report": f"Report: Research={state['research']}, Analysis={state['analysis']}, Viz={state['visualization']}"}

graph = StateGraph(State)
graph.add_node("research", research_agent)
graph.add_node("analysis", analysis_agent)
graph.add_node("visualization", visualization_agent)
graph.add_node("report", report_agent)

graph.add_edge(START, "research")
graph.add_edge("research", "analysis")
graph.add_edge("analysis", "visualization")
graph.add_edge("visualization", "report")
graph.add_edge("report", END)

app = graph.compile()

# Test: One agent crash kills the entire pipeline
result = app.invoke({"topic": "AI trends 2025", "research": "", "analysis": "", "visualization": "", "report": ""})
print(result["report"])
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew

researcher = Agent(
    role="Research Specialist",
    goal="Research the given topic thoroughly",
    backstory="You are an expert researcher with deep knowledge.",
    verbose=True,
)

analyst = Agent(
    role="Data Analyst",
    goal="Analyze the research findings",
    backstory="You are a data analyst who finds insights in research.",
    verbose=True,
)

visualizer = Agent(
    role="Visualization Expert",
    goal="Create visual representations of the analysis",
    backstory="You turn data into compelling visuals.",
    verbose=True,
)

reporter = Agent(
    role="Report Writer",
    goal="Compile everything into a final report",
    backstory="You write clear, comprehensive reports.",
    verbose=True,
)

research_task = Task(
    description="Research AI trends for 2025",
    agent=researcher,
    expected_output="Research findings",
)

# BUG: If any task fails, the entire crew crashes with no partial results
analysis_task = Task(
    description="Analyze the research (NOTE: this will fail due to a simulated error)",
    agent=analyst,
    expected_output="Analysis results",
)

viz_task = Task(
    description="Create visualizations from the analysis",
    agent=visualizer,
    expected_output="Visualization descriptions",
)

report_task = Task(
    description="Write the final report combining all outputs",
    agent=reporter,
    expected_output="Final report",
)

crew = Crew(
    agents=[researcher, analyst, visualizer, reporter],
    tasks=[research_task, analysis_task, viz_task, report_task],
    verbose=True,
)

# Test: One agent failure crashes the entire crew
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

research_agent = Agent(
    name="Research Specialist",
    instructions="You are an expert researcher. Research the given topic thoroughly.",
)

analysis_agent = Agent(
    name="Data Analyst",
    instructions="You are a data analyst who finds insights in research.",
)

visualization_agent = Agent(
    name="Visualization Expert",
    instructions="You turn data into compelling visuals.",
)

report_agent = Agent(
    name="Report Writer",
    instructions="You write clear, comprehensive reports.",
)

# BUG: If any agent crashes, the entire pipeline crashes with no partial results
agents_config = [
    ("research", research_agent, "Research AI trends for 2025"),
    ("analysis", analysis_agent, "Analyze the research (NOTE: this will fail due to a simulated error)"),
    ("visualization", visualization_agent, "Create visualizations for AI trends"),
    ("report", report_agent, "Write the final report combining all outputs"),
]

results = {}
for name, agent, prompt in agents_config:
    if name == "analysis":
        raise RuntimeError("Analysis model failed to load")
    result = Runner.run_sync(agent, prompt)
    results[name] = result.final_output

print("Final Results:")
for name, output in results.items():
    print(f"\n{name.title()}: {output}")

evaluationCriteria.label: Failure is isolated to one agent
evaluationCriteria.description: A crash in one agent does not propagate to or stop other agents

evaluationCriteria.label: Other agents continue running
evaluationCriteria.description: Agents that are not affected by the failure still execute and produce results

evaluationCriteria.label: Partial results returned
evaluationCriteria.description: The final output includes results from all successful agents

evaluationCriteria.label: Failed agent is reported
evaluationCriteria.description: The output clearly notes which agent failed and that its contribution is missing
